theme: Windows security hardening and tvOS SKSurface fixes
highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 3.119.2 hardens the Windows and Linux native binaries with Control Flow Guard, BufferSecurityCheck and Spectre mitigation.
highlights_body: It also fills in missing `SKSurface.Create` overloads for tvOS and preserves Android symbols in the packages so crash reports remain useful.

bullets.detail: Adds the missing `SKSurface.Create` overloads that had been dropping from the tvOS target so callers no longer have to reach through a workaround to create surfaces on tvOS.

bullets.lead: Android debug symbols packed with the package
bullets.detail: Preserves and packs the Android native symbols so crash dumps and profilers get real function names instead of stripped stubs.

bullets.lead: Windows DLLs built with Control Flow Guard and BufferSecurityCheck
bullets.detail: All Windows native DLLs now ship with Control Flow Guard (`/guard:cf`) and buffer-security checks (`/GS`) enabled, resolving BinSkim BA2007 and hardening indirect calls at runtime.
The Windows `libSkiaSharp.dll` is now compiled with the Spectre mitigation flag to reduce the impact of speculative-execution side-channel attacks on consuming apps.

contributor_summaries.Aguilex: Enabled Control Flow Guard and BufferSecurityCheck on the Windows native DLLs

preview_summaries.p3: Preview 3 added the Spectre mitigation compiler flag to `libSkiaSharp.dll` on Windows.
preview_summaries.p2: Preview 2 enabled Control Flow Guard and BufferSecurityCheck for every Windows native DLL and folded in the docs and release-skills work.
preview_summaries.p1: Preview 1 cut the 3.119.2 line, restored the missing tvOS `SKSurface.Create` overloads and preserved the Android native symbols in the package.
